overview.ideaSummary: Stallio is a two-sided marketplace where local makers list products and buyers discover, message, and purchase with ratings and simple payouts.
overview.problemStatement: Local makers lack a trustworthy place to sell without Amazon fees, while buyers struggle to find verified nearby goods. Generic classifieds lack checkout and dispute basics.
overview.valueProposition: Stallio connects local makers and buyers with listings, search, checkout, and lightweight trust tools—without marketplace mega-fee complexity.
coreFeaturesOverview: Seller listings with photos and inventory
Search and filters by category and distance
Seller dashboard for orders and payouts
overview.userJourneySummary: Seller creates a listing; buyer searches, messages, checks out; order and payout tracked; both leave reviews.

steps: Step 1: Seller onboards and creates a listing.
Step 2: Buyer searches and opens listing detail.

screenDescriptions.navigationStructure: Top: Browse, Messages, Sell, Account. Checkout is a focused flow.

personas.name: Mina
personas.jtbd: When I finish a batch, I want listings and orders in one place.

personas.name: Omar
personas.jtbd: When I search locally, I want verified sellers and clear checkout.

personas.name: Priya
personas.jtbd: When a dispute opens, I want status and evidence in one queue.

stories.title: Create listing
stories.description: As Mina, I publish a listing with photos and price.
acceptanceCriteria: Listing appears in Browse when active

stories.title: Complete checkout
stories.description: As Omar, I pay for a listing.

stories.title: Leave review
stories.description: As Omar, I rate a completed order.

successMetrics.northStar: Weekly completed orders with review rate above target

glossary.definition: A sellable product or service entry.
A paid purchase between buyer and seller.
Transfer of funds to seller after fulfillment window.

architectureOverview.narrative: Browser SPA talks to Stallio API. API owns orders, macros, and SLA state in Postgres. An email worker syncs IMAP/OAuth mail and enqueues inbound messages. Object storage holds attachments.
architectureOverview.systemContextDiagramMermaid:
flowchart LR
  User["Buyer or Seller"] --> SPA["Stallio Web"]
  SPA --> API["Stallio API"]
  API --> DB[(Postgres)]
  API --> Store[(Object Storage)]
  Worker["Orders Worker"] --> API
  Worker --> Mail["Payments"]
  API --> Mail

databaseSchema.description: Core entities: workspace, user, mailbox, order, message, macro, sla_policy.
databaseSchema.erDiagramMermaid:
erDiagram
  WORKSPACE ||--o{ USER : has
  WORKSPACE ||--|| CATALOG : owns
  WORKSPACE ||--o{ ORDER : contains
  ORDER ||--o{ MESSAGE : includes
  WORKSPACE ||--o{ LISTING : defines
  WORKSPACE ||--|| SLA_POLICY : configures
  USER ||--o{ ORDER : assigned
  WORKSPACE {
    uuid id PK
    string name
  }
  ORDER {
    uuid id PK
    string status
    datetime sla_due_at
  }

tablesSupplement.name: orders
tablesSupplement.relationships: belongs to workspace; optional assignee user

apiDesign.endpointsOverviewDiagramMermaid:
flowchart TD
  Client["SPA"] --> Auth["POST /auth/magic-link"]
  Client --> Inbox["GET /orders"]
  Client --> Reply["POST /orders/{id}/replies"]
  Client --> Listing templates["CRUD /macros"]

endpoints.description: List orders for workspace with filters
Send a reply on a order

dataFlow.narrative: Inbound mail hits worker, normalized to message rows, order upserted, SLA due set. Outbound replies go API to provider send then message append.
dataFlow.diagramMermaid:
flowchart TD
  In["Checkout event"] --> W["Orders Worker"]
  W --> API["API ingest"]
  API --> DB[(Postgres)]
  Agent["Agent reply"] --> API2["POST replies"]
  API2 --> Provider["Mail send"]
  API2 --> DB

securityAuthentication.authentication: Magic link email OTP issues session cookie
securityAuthentication.encryption: TLS in transit; encrypted-at-rest DB volumes
securityAuthentication.securityMeasures: CSRF on cookie session, attachment MIME allowlist, rate limits
securityCriticalPoints: Never store mail provider refresh tokens in localStorage
Sanitize HTML email bodies before render
Idempotency keys on outbound send to prevent duplicate customer emails
Restrict attachment types and max size
securityAuthentication.authFlowDiagramMermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as User
  participant SPA as SPA
  participant API as API
  U->>SPA: Request magic link
  SPA->>API: POST /auth/magic-link
  API-->>U: Email with token
  U->>SPA: Open link
  SPA->>API: POST /auth/verify
  API-->>SPA: Set session cookie

integrationExternalApis.integrations: Email sync worker + object storage for attachments
integrationExternalApis.dataFlow: OAuth tokens stored server-side; attachments streamed to object storage
integrationExternalApis.integrationLandscapeDiagramMermaid:
flowchart LR
  API["API"] --> OAuth["Mail OAuth"]
  API --> SMTP["SMTP send"]
  API --> S3["Object storage"]
  Worker["Worker"] --> OAuth

devops.deploymentStrategy: Containerized API + worker on a single PaaS; managed Postgres
devops.infrastructure: API service, worker service, Postgres, object storage, Redis for rate limit/idempotency
devops.monitoring: Request latency, email sync lag, SLA breach count
devops.scaling: Horizontal API; worker concurrency capped by provider limits
devops.automation: CI runs lint, unit, migrate dry-run
devops.cicdPipelineDiagramMermaid:
flowchart TD
  Push["git push"] --> CI["CI lint test"]
  CI --> Migrate["migrate"]
  Migrate --> Deploy["deploy API and worker"]

dataStorage.storageStrategy: Postgres primary; object storage for attachments
dataStorage.dataRetention: Resolved orders retained 24 months; raw MIME 90 days
dataStorage.storageArchitecture: Relational source of truth; blob store for files

analytics.analyticsStrategy: First-party product analytics for funnel and SLA
analytics.trackingMethods: Server events for reply_sent, macro_used, sla_breached
analytics.reporting: Owner dashboard: median first response, breach rate

eventCatalog.name: order.created
eventCatalog.description: Fired when inbound mail creates a order

technical.migrationAndSeed: SQL migrations via node-pg-migrate. Seed: demo workspace, two users, three sample orders, two macros.

performanceBudgets.notes: Inbox list p95 under 400ms excluding mail provider

mvpCostModel.assumptions: Estimate for ~3 users, 2k orders/month — labeled estimate not a quote

testPlan.unit: Order status transitions, SLA due calculation, macro variable interpolation
testPlan.integration: API auth + reply idempotency with test DB
testPlan.e2e: Connect mailbox mock, receive, reply, resolve
testPlan.security: HTML sanitization fixtures; CSRF negative tests

industryOverview.trends: Indie SaaS teams favor lightweight support tools over enterprise suites; flat pricing and shared inboxes are rising themes.
industryOverview.marketData: Estimate (directional): early-stage B2B SaaS increasingly runs founder-led support before hiring a full CX team. Not a precise TAM figure.
industryOverview.growthProjections: Estimate: continued demand for simple shared inboxes as more solo founders ship products.
industryOverview.growthPotential: Strong among bootstrapped SaaS; weaker among enterprises needing omnichannel.
industryOverview.estimateDisclaimer: All figures and sizes are qualitative estimates, not audited market research.

primaryAudience.description: Indie SaaS founders doing their own support
primaryAudience.goals: Respond fast without hiring a CX team
primaryAudience.painPoints: Gmail chaos, no SLA visibility, seat-tax tools

secondaryAudience.description: Part-time support teammates and technical co-founders

targetAudienceInsights.needsAnalysis: Need shared truth for email, ownership, and simple urgency signals.
targetAudienceInsights.usageHabits: Check support between product work; mobile triage occasionally.
targetAudienceInsights.demographics: Global English-speaking local makers; small teams 1-5.

threats: AI inbox features in email clients

monetizationModel.pricingStrategy: Flat monthly workspace plan plus optional attachment storage pack
monetizationModel.rationale: Avoid seat tax which punishes adding a helper
monetizationModel.recommendations: Launch with one clear Pro plan
monetizationModel.methodology: Anchored to indie willingness-to-pay interviews and competitor seat math — not a fabricated ROI %

marketingStrategy.channels: Indie Hackers, Twitter/X founders, Product Hunt, SEO for shared inbox

questions: How do you handle support@ today?
When did the last customer email get lost?
What would you pay monthly to fix that?
What replies do you repeat weekly?
How do you know what is urgent?

market.positioningOnePager: For local makers teams who outgrow shared Gmail, Stallio is the shared inbox that adds assignment, macros, and Fulfillment SLA without enterprise seat tax.

visualStyleGuide.colorHarmony: Warm orange primary on cream background with near-black text; teal success and coral danger for semantic clarity. Accent is a lighter orange for hover/focus.
visualStyleGuide.colorReasoning: Orange signals speed and approachability for support workflows; cream reduces glare for long inbox sessions; high-contrast text meets AA on surfaces.

typography.body: IBM Plex Sans for body and UI

visualStyleGuide.buttons: Primary filled orange; secondary outline; destructive coral text button
visualStyleGuide.animations: 150-200ms ease-out for hover; 250ms for panel slide

logoIconography.logoConcepts: Wordmark Stallio with a small inbox chevron mark
logoIconography.colorVersions: Full color on cream; mono white on dark; mono dark on light
logoIconography.iconSet: Stroke icons 1.5px: inbox, macro, clock, settings

uiLayout.landingPage: Full-bleed cream hero with product inbox screenshot as atmosphere; one headline and CTA
uiLayout.dashboard: Inbox split view: list left, order right
uiLayout.navigation: Top bar with Inbox, Listing templates, Settings
uiLayout.responsiveDesign: Stack to single column under 768px; order becomes full route

uxPrinciples.userFlow: Connect email first, then inbox; macros secondary
uxPrinciples.accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA contrast; focus rings; keyboard order nav
uxPrinciples.informationHierarchy: SLA breach and unassigned float above resolved noise

screenLayouts.layout: Filter bar + virtualized list + optional split preview
Header meta, scroll timeline, sticky composer

motionSpecs.name: Order open
motionSpecs.notes: Slide panel in from right on desktop

motionSpecs.name: SLA breach pulse
motionSpecs.notes: Single subtle pulse; respect reduced motion

design.toneOfVoice: Clear, calm, founder-friendly. Short sentences. No enterprise jargon.

microcopy.copy: Your queue is clear. Nice work—or connect email to start.

architecture.executiveSummary: Stallio is a small-system marketplace: SPA, API, email worker, Postgres, and object storage. Critical areas are email sync reliability, reply idempotency, and HTML sanitization of inbound mail.
architecture.systemBoundaries: In scope: shared inbox, macros, Fulfillment SLA, magic-link auth. Out of scope: omnichannel, public KB, WFM. Trust boundary at API; mail provider is external.

logicalSystemArchitecture.narrative: Presentation (SPA), application API, async email worker, persistence (Postgres + object storage), and external mail/identity providers.
logicalSystemArchitecture.diagramMermaid:
flowchart TB
  subgraph Client
    SPA["Web SPA"]
  end
  subgraph Stallio
    API["API"]
    Worker["Orders Worker"]
    DB[(Postgres)]
    Blob[(Object Storage)]
  end
  SPA --> API
  Worker --> API
  API --> DB
  API --> Blob
  Worker --> Mail["Payments"]
  API --> Mail

informationArchitecture.narrative: Workspace is the tenancy root. Users, mailbox, orders/messages, macros, and sla_policy hang off workspace.
informationArchitecture.diagramMermaid:
flowchart LR
  WS["Workspace"] --> Users["Users"]
  WS --> Catalog["Catalog"]
  WS --> Orders["Orders"]
  Orders --> Messages["Messages"]
  WS --> Listing templates["Listing templates"]
  WS --> SLA["SLA Policy"]

functionalArchitecture.narrative: Modules: Auth, Inbox, Composer/Listing templates, SLA, Email Sync, Admin/Settings.
functionalArchitecture.diagramMermaid:
flowchart TD
  Auth["Auth"] --> Inbox["Inbox"]
  Inbox --> Composer["Composer"]
  Composer --> Listing templates["Listing templates"]
  Inbox --> SLA["SLA Lite"]
  Sync["Email Sync"] --> Inbox
  Settings["Settings"] --> Auth
  Settings --> Sync

architecture.repositoryStructure:
stallio/
  apps/web/          # Vite React SPA
  apps/api/          # Express API
  apps/worker/       # Email sync worker
  packages/shared/   # Types and validators
  docs/architecture/ # ADRs and runbooks

coreFlows.narrative: Primary flow: inbound email creates order with SLA due; agent replies with optional macro; status updates.
coreFlows.primarySequenceDiagramMermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant M as MailProvider
  participant W as Worker
  participant API as API
  participant A as Agent
  M->>W: New email
  W->>API: Ingest message
  API->>API: Upsert order and SLA
  A->>API: POST reply
  API->>M: Send email
  API->>A: 201 message

integrationLandscape.narrative: Mail OAuth/IMAP, transactional email for magic links, S3-compatible storage.
integrationLandscape.diagramMermaid:
flowchart LR
  API["API"] --> OAuth["Mail OAuth"]
  API --> Tx["Transactional email"]
  API --> S3["Object storage"]
  Worker["Worker"] --> OAuth

deploymentTopology.narrative: Single region PaaS: API and worker services, managed Postgres, object storage, Redis for idempotency keys.
deploymentTopology.diagramMermaid:
flowchart TB
  Edge["HTTPS edge"] --> API["API service"]
  Edge --> Static["SPA static"]
  API --> DB[(Postgres)]
  API --> Redis[(Redis)]
  Worker["Worker service"] --> DB
  Worker --> Mail["Payments"]

architecture.nonFunctionalQuality: Availability target 99.5% monthly for API. Inbox p95 400ms. Email sync lag alert at 10 minutes. Attachments max 10MB.
architecture.observabilityOperability: Structured logs with workspaceId; metrics: sync_lag_seconds, reply_send_failures, sla_breach_count; traces on ingest and send.
architecture.securityArchitectureDeepDive: Session cookies HttpOnly Secure SameSite=Lax; CSRF tokens on mutating routes; HTML sanitizer allowlist; secrets in env only; least-privilege DB roles.
architecture.architectureDecisionLog: See structured adrs[] for magic-link auth, outbox events, and HTML sanitization.
architecture.risksAndOpenDecisions: IMAP vs API-first providers; multi-mailbox later; whether to add webhooks for order.created externally.
architecture.contextDiagramMermaid:
flowchart LR
  Founder["Founder"] --> System["Stallio"]
  Agent["Agent"] --> System
  System --> Mail["Payments"]
  System --> Store["Object Storage"]
  System --> TxMail["Magic-link email"]
architecture.containerDiagramMermaid:
flowchart TB
  SPA["SPA container"] --> API["API container"]
  Worker["Worker container"] --> API
  API --> PG[(Postgres)]
  API --> Obj[(Object storage)]
  API --> Redis[(Redis)]

adrs.title: Magic-link sessions instead of passwords
adrs.context: Indie users hate password reset loops; support tool login must be low friction.
adrs.decision: Magic link issuing HttpOnly session cookies
adrs.revisitWhen: Enterprise SSO demand appears or email deliverability is chronically poor

adrs.title: Transactional outbox for domain events
adrs.context: Need reliable analytics/notifications without dual-write bugs.

adrs.title: Strict HTML sanitization on inbound mail
adrs.context: Email HTML is a XSS vector in the agent UI.
adrs.decision: Server-side sanitizer with conservative allowlist plus CSP

threatModel.mitigation: Sanitize on ingest; CSP; escape in React
Secure HttpOnly; short TTL; revoke on logout

failureModes.runbook: Notify owner; Settings reconnect OAuth; backfill last 24h
Rely on managed failover; pause worker; verify migrations

architecture.repoToPromptsMap: Stage 1 maps to apps/web+api scaffolding; Stage 2 Inbox UI in apps/web; Stage 4 API orders/macros; Stage 7 mail OAuth; Stage 9 tests; Stage 10 deploy worker+API.

visibility.strategySummary: Position Stallio as the indie shared inbox: SEO for shared-inbox intent, GEO assets (llms.txt/JSON-LD), and a steady founder-content engine.

seoFoundation.positioning: Shared inbox for local makers without seat tax

prompts.fullPrompt:
You are building Stallio Local Marketplace - Stallio is a two-sided marketplace where local makers list products and buyers discover, message, and purchase with ratings and simple payouts.

PROJECT OVERVIEW:
Stallio connects local makers and buyers with listings, search, checkout, and lightweight trust tools—without marketplace mega-fee complexity.

TECHNICAL STACK:
- Frontend: React 18 + Vite + TypeScript
- Backend: Node.js 20 + Express
- Database: PostgreSQL 16
- Authentication: Email magic link + session cookies (HttpOnly)

DEVELOPMENT STAGES - BUILD IN THIS EXACT ORDER:
Each stage below is an Agent Ticket with Goal, Non-goals, Files, AC, Commands, DoD, Pitfalls, and Build/Review/Tests variants.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
STAGE 1: PROJECT SETUP & BASIC STRUCTURE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## Goal
Scaffold Stallio apps for browse and seller flows.

## Non-goals
- Payments live
- Moderation AI

## Files to create
- apps/web/package.json
- apps/api/src/index.ts
- packages/shared/src/types.ts

## Files to modify
- Root package.json workspaces

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Health 200
- [ ] Browse shell route
- [ ] Sell shell route

## Commands
```bash
pnpm install
pnpm --filter api dev
pnpm --filter web dev
```

## Definition of done
- README
- CI lint

## Pitfalls
- Secrets only on API

## Variant: Build
1.1 Workspaces
1.2 Health
1.3 Shell routes

## Variant: Review
- No secrets in web

## Variant: Tests
- Health unit

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
STAGE 2: LISTINGS & SEARCH
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## Goal
Listing CRUD and search API with Browse UI.

## Non-goals
- Payments
- Reviews

## Files to create
- apps/api/src/routes/listings.ts
- apps/web/src/pages/BrowsePage.tsx

## Files to modify
- packages/shared/src/types.ts

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Create listing as seller
- [ ] Search returns filters
- [ ] Empty state

## Commands
```bash
pnpm --filter api test
pnpm --filter web test -- BrowsePage
```

## Definition of done
- Empty/error states

## Pitfalls
- Paginate search

## Variant: Build
2.1 Listing model
2.2 Search
2.3 Browse UI

## Variant: Review
- Auth on create

## Variant: Tests
- Search filter

instructions: Set redirect URL to API callback
